headline: Dayak Identity Politics Reshapes Sarawak's 2026 Electoral Landscape
context: A coalition of Dayak political movements is demanding 40% of Sarawak state seats reflect Dayak population share, up from the current 32%, ahead of the 2026 state election.

cards.big: Dayak peoples are 42% of Sarawak's population.
cards.sub: They hold 32% of state assembly seats and 3 of 13 Cabinet positions. The gap is 10 percentage points.

cards.big: Electoral constituency delineation has not been reviewed since 2015.
cards.sub: Urban migration by Dayak communities is not reflected in current boundaries.

cards.big: Dayak household income is 24% below the Sarawak average.
cards.sub: Political underrepresentation correlates with economic underperformance across all Dayak sub-groups.

cards.big: GPS coalition allocates seats through party negotiation, not demographic proportionality.
cards.sub: Dayak seats are determined by political horse-trading, not population share.

cards.big: 42% of the people, 32% of the seats. In a democracy, numbers should determine representation. In Sarawak, parties do.

Proportional representation is a democratic principle. If Dayak communities are 42% of Sarawak, they should hold approximately 42% of seats. Achieving this requires constituency reform, not just coalition negotiation.
